title: trachyspermum involucratum
intro: Trachyspermum involucratum, also known as Ajwain or Bishop's Weed, is a small, annual herb native to the Indian subcontinent. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and carminative effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Trachyspermum involucratum include relief from nausea, bloating, and gas, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Trachyspermum involucratum include teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to treat a variety of ailments and promote overall well-being.
